Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to defend HTML, CSV, paths, archives, logs, terminals, and plugins against untrusted crawler content; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then change one duplicate, accessibility state, axe result, resource budget, browser readiness, or manual-review condition without overclaiming. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Render findings through text-safe sinks or context-specific encoding and narrowly reviewed sanitization.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Safe Rendering Sinks model · Theory Safe Renderi…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Escaping HTML once makes content safe in every output context.
